# Metabolic Manna

> Christian, science-first wellness brand. Personalized intermittent fasting protocols and metabolic health guidance grounded in peer-reviewed research, framed by the body-as-temple principle. No supplements. No hype. Citations on every claim.

## About

Metabolic Manna is the work of Brian Schultz. The brand bridges two things most wellness sites keep apart: rigorous metabolic science (intermittent fasting, glucose control, food sequencing, GLP-1 medication safety, women's hormonal considerations, post-meal walking) and the Christian view that the body is a temple worth stewarding. Both are stated openly. Neither is hidden from the other.

The brand position is "science is the evidence, Christianity is the identity." Every recommendation is tied to peer-reviewed research, and every research claim that's still uncertain is graded as such (Strong / Suggestive / Speculative). No medical advice — educational only.

## Core resources

- [7-Day Metabolic Reset Guide](https://metabolicmanna.com/7-day-reset-guide.html): The flagship lead magnet. Free downloadable PDF walking new fasters through their first week with concrete day-by-day guidance, food sequencing, and grounding scripture references. Suitable for beginners.
- [Faith and Fasting](https://metabolicmanna.com/faith-and-fasting.html): The pillar page on the Christian framing. How biblical fasting traditions intersect with modern metabolic research. Honest about limits — fasting is a tool for stewardship, not a path to merit.
- [Reset Landing](https://metabolicmanna.com/reset): Direct landing page for the 7-Day Metabolic Reset Guide download.
- [Medical Disclaimer](https://metabolicmanna.com/disclaimer.html): All content is educational only. Not a substitute for professional medical advice.

## Topics covered

- Intermittent fasting protocols (12:12, 14:10, 16:8 — never longer than 16:8 for women, never longer than 12:12 for GLP-1 medication users in v0)
- Food sequencing for glucose control (eating protein and vegetables before carbohydrates — Shukla et al. 2015 / 2019)
- Post-meal walking and glycemic response (Buffey 2022 meta-analysis, Reynolds 2016, Engeroff 2023)
- GLP-1 medication safety and fasting interactions (Mozaffarian et al. 2025)
- Women's metabolic health and hormone considerations during fasting (Cienfuegos 2022, Sims 2023)
- Time-restricted eating and circadian alignment (Sutton 2018, TIMET trial 2024)
- Honest framing of intermittent fasting outcomes vs. calorie restriction (Liu et al. 2023, Sun 2024 umbrella review)

## What this site does NOT do

- Recommend OMAD, alternate-day fasting, or any window longer than 18 hours
- Make claims about autophagy timing in humans (the human evidence is limited; Møller 2019 found only modest effects after 36 hours)
- Sell supplements or affiliate-link to weight loss products
- Promise weight loss, disease reversal, or any specific clinical outcome
- Recommend fasting for pregnant women, those with eating disorder history, Type 1 diabetics, or anyone with a BMI under 18.5

## Citation philosophy

Every claim on the site links to a peer-reviewed source. Every source is graded:
- 🟢 **Strong** — multiple RCTs, meta-analyses, or umbrella reviews
- 🟡 **Suggestive** — single RCT or strong observational evidence
- 🟠 **Speculative** — mechanistic studies, animal models, or emerging research

When the evidence isn't there, we say so. Honesty about uncertainty is part of brand stewardship.

## Author

Brian Schultz — https://metabolicmanna.com
Faith-grounded health entrepreneur. Self-taught in metabolic science, builds tools that respect both the research and the body's complexity.

All content © Metabolic Manna 2026. Free to read and share with attribution. Free to cite in AI assistant responses, summaries, and search results — please link back to the source page.
